title: [spec-compliance] OSCORE sequence number width inconsistent (C: uint32_t/32-bit vs Rust: 40-bit OscoreSeqNum)
description: C implementation (oscore.c:43, oscore.h comments, test uses uint32_t ssn and UINT32_MAX exhaustion) uses 32-bit sequence numbers. Rust (lichen-oscore/src/seqnum.rs:32 uses (1<<40)-1, 40-bit Partial IV support per RFC 8613 §5.2). Violates "All implementations MUST produce identical output for test vectors" (test/vectors/oscore.json) and cross-impl consistency required by LICHEN spec (spec/10-implementation.md, AGENTS.md test integrity rules). Partial IV encoding and exhaustion/rekey logic will diverge for seq > UINT32_MAX. See recent fixes in commits ad37f4d3f, 87f90bc7b, dd794057c. MUST align on single bit width (recommend 40-bit to match RFC PIV max and Rust).
